briefs.title: Proposed: Five new non-residential zoning districts for Burnaby
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on proposed amendments to the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw that would create five new zoning districts for non-residential properties (like commercial, industrial, and mixed-use areas). These changes are part of the Burnaby 2050 Official Community Plan (OCP) — the city's long-term land use plan. If approved, the new districts would replace some existing zones and affect what types of businesses and buildings are allowed in certain areas. The proposal has not been finalized yet; a public hearing or council vote is still needed.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public hearing date — this is your chance to comment on how the new zones could affect your property or business.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Retail
briefs.source_body:
7.1
PROPOSED BYLAW AMENDMENTS - ZONING BYLAW REWRITE PROJECT - REVISED NEW NON-RESIDENTIAL ZONING DISTRICTS
Purpose: To propose revised amendments to the Burnaby Zoning Bylaw to establish five new zoning districts and related changes to implement the Burnaby 2050 OCP land use framework.

briefs.title: Proposed: Remove restaurant-only rule at 8340 Fraser Reach Court
briefs.summary: Burnaby Council is set to vote on a proposal to remove a legal restriction (called a Section 219 restrictive covenant) on a property at 8340 Fraser Reach Court. This covenant currently says a portion of the land can only be used as a restaurant. If approved, the restriction would be lifted, allowing the owner to use that part of the property for other purposes. This is a proposed change — it has not been decided yet.
briefs.action_required: If you own or operate a business near this property, watch for the council decision — a change in use could affect nearby traffic, parking, or competition.
briefs.industries: Food Service, Construction & Development, Landlord & Property Mgmt
briefs.source_body:
7.2
PROPOSED COVENANT DISCHARGE AT 8340 FRASER REACH COURT (REZ #14-31)
Purpose: To seek authorization to discharge a Section 219 restrictive covenant on title, which restricts the use of a portion of the subject property for restaurant use only.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to update council meeting rules (Procedure Bylaw)
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on proposed amendments to the Burnaby Procedure Bylaw. This bylaw sets the rules for how council meetings are run, how decisions are made, and how the public can participate. If approved, the changes could affect how and when you can speak at council meetings or submit comments on issues that matter to your business. The exact details of the proposed changes are not yet public, but council will consider bringing them forward for a vote at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the public notice of the proposed bylaw amendments — you may want to review the changes and speak at a public hearing.
briefs.source_body:
7.3
BURNABY PROCEDURE BYLAW UPDATE
Purpose:
To seek authorization to bring forward amendments to Burnaby Procedure Bylaw 2024.

briefs.title: Council to decide on Lochdale School Park playground replacement
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider the future of the Lochdale School Park playground. A report on the agenda asks council for direction on whether to replace the playground equipment. This is a proposed decision only — no final choice has been made yet. The outcome could affect local families and community groups who use the park.
briefs.source_body:
7.4
LOCHDALE SCHOOL PARK PLAYGROUND
Purpose:
To seek direction on the future of the Lochdale School Park playground replacement.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby to award contract for two split-body refuse trucks
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a contract award for the supply and delivery of two new split-body refuse trucks. A split-body refuse truck has two separate compartments, allowing it to collect garbage and recycling in one trip. If approved, this would replace aging trucks in the city's fleet. The decision is on the agenda for the upcoming council meeting.
briefs.source_body:
7.5
CA - TWO (2) SPLIT BODY REFUSE TRUCKS
Purpose:
T
o obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of two (2) Split Body Refuse Trucks.

briefs.title: Proposed: Council to vote on contract for new garbage truck
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a contract award for the supply and delivery of a new tandem axle front load refuse truck. This truck would be used for collecting garbage. The decision is not final yet — council will consider and vote on the proposed contract at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.source_body:
7.6
CA - TANDEM AXLE FRONT LOAD REFUSE TRUCK
Purpose:
T
o obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of a Tandem Axle Front Load Refuse Truck.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on fuel supply contract award
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on awarding a contract for the supply and delivery of gasoline, diesel, bio-diesel, and related fuel products. If approved, this contract would secure fuel for city vehicles and equipment. The decision is on the upcoming council meeting agenda and has not yet been finalized.
briefs.source_body:
7.7
CA - SUPPLY AND DELIVERY OF FUEL PRODUCTS
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Award (CA) for the supply and delivery of gasoline, diesel, bio-diesel and related fuel products.

briefs.title: Council to vote on contract change for three Toyota Tacoma trucks
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change (a formal request to modify an existing purchasing agreement) to buy three additional Toyota Tacoma pickup trucks. If approved, this would increase the number of trucks the city is getting under the current supply and delivery contract. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been finalized.
briefs.source_body:
7.8
CC - THREE (3) TOYOTA TACOMA PICKUP TRUCKS
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for the supply and delivery of three (3) additional Toyota Tacoma Pickup Trucks.

briefs.title: Proposed: Council to approve contract change for archaeological consulting services
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change (a modification to an existing agreement) for archaeological consulting services. This change would adjust the terms or budget of a current contract with an archaeological consultant. The decision has not been made yet — it is up for approval at an upcoming council meeting.
briefs.source_body:
7.9
CC - ARCHAEOLOGICAL CONSULTING SERVICES
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for archaeological consulting services.

briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for Housekeeping Services Better at Home Program
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on approving a contract change for the Housekeeping Services Better at Home (BAH) Program. This proposed change would modify the existing agreement for housekeeping services provided to seniors and people with disabilities who need help staying in their own homes. The decision is not final — Council will consider and vote on this proposal at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.source_body:
7.10
CC - HOUSEKEEPING SERVICES BETTER AT HOME PROGRAM
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval to award a Contract Change (CC) for Housekeeping Services Better at Home (BAH) Program.

briefs.title: Proposed: Contract change for hazardous material surveys at city sites
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposed contract change (an amendment to an existing agreement) for Pre-Project Hazardous Building Material Survey Services at various City of Burnaby locations. This means the city wants to adjust the terms or cost of a contract with a company that checks for dangerous materials like asbestos before construction or renovation projects begin. The decision has not been made yet — council will discuss and vote on whether to approve this change.
briefs.source_body:
7.11
CC - HAZARDOUS BUILDING MATERIAL SURVEY SERVICES
Purpose: To obtain Council approval for a Contract Change (CC) for Pre-Project Hazardous Building Material Survey Services at various City Locations.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to vote on one-year E-COMM police dispatch contract renewal
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a one-year contract renewal for police dispatch services provided by E-COMM. This is not a new service — it continues the existing arrangement for another year. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming meeting and has not yet been approved. If passed, the renewal would keep emergency police dispatch running without interruption.
briefs.source_body:
7.12
CR - E-COMM POLICE DISPATCH SERVICES
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a one-year Contract Renewal (CR) for police dispatch services.

briefs.title: Council to vote on fence rental contract renewal
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on renewing a contract for the supply and delivery of fence rental services. If approved, this would extend the city's existing agreement with a fence rental company. This is a routine procurement decision that keeps the city's operations running smoothly.
briefs.source_body:
7.13
CR - SUPPLY AND DELIVERY OF FENCE RENTAL SERVICES
Purpose:
To obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for the Supply and Delivery of Fence Rental Services
.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby council to vote on road marking paint contract renewal
briefs.summary: Council will consider approving a contract renewal for the supply and delivery of road marking paint and glass beads. This is a routine renewal of an existing contract, not a new program. If approved, it would ensure the city has the materials needed to paint and maintain road lines and markings. The decision is on the agenda for an upcoming council meeting and has not yet been made.
briefs.source_body:
7.14
CR - SUPPLY AND DELIVERY OF ROAD MARKING PAINT AND GLASS BEAD
Purpose:
T
o obtain Council approval for a Contract Renewal (CR) for the Supply and Delivery of Road Marking Paint & Glass Bead.

briefs.title: Proposed: Council to approve PRCC members' attendance at BCRPA Symposium
briefs.summary: Council is set to vote on a proposal to allow members of the Parks, Recreation and Culture Commission (PRCC) to attend the BC Recreation and Parks Association (BCRPA) Symposium. The proposal asks Council to approve spending money for this trip. If approved, PRCC members would be able to go to the symposium, which is a conference for people who work in parks and recreation. This is not a final decision yet — Council will discuss and vote on it at an upcoming meeting.
briefs.source_body:
8.1
PRCC - 2026 BCRPA SYMPOSIUM
Purpose: To seek Council approval of an expenditure for the PRCC members to attend the BC Recreation and Parks Association (BCRPA) Symposium.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Council to receive December 2025 building permit report
briefs.summary: Council will consider a report summarizing building permits issued in Burnaby from December 1 to December 31, 2025. This is an information-only item — no vote on new rules or fees is proposed. The report gives a snapshot of construction activity in the city for that month.
briefs.source_body:
9.1
SU: BUILDING PERMIT ACTIVITY REPORT NO. 12 FROM DECEMBER 1 - DECEMBER 31, 2025
Purpose: To provide Council with information on construction activity as reflected by the building permits that have been issued for the subject period.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan Phase 3 update
briefs.summary: Council will receive a status update on Phase 3 of the Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan. This phase involves drafting the plan and preparing for upcoming community engagement sessions. The plan will guide future parks, recreation, and culture services in Burnaby. No decisions have been made yet — this is a progress report to council.
briefs.source_body:
9.2
PRCC - SU: PRC PLAN: PHASE 3
Purpose: To provide a status update (SU) on the Parks, Recreation and Culture Plan Phase 3: Drafting and Forthcoming Community Engagement.

briefs.title: Proposed: Burnaby 2026 park development projects and timeline
briefs.summary: Council will consider a report on the Parks, Recreation and Culture (PRC) department's proposed park development projects for 2026. This includes a list of specific parks to be built or upgraded, along with a timeline for when each project would start and finish. The report is for information only at this stage — no final decision has been made. If approved, these projects could affect local parks, trails, and recreation facilities in your neighbourhood.
briefs.source_body:
9.3
PRCC - PRC 2026 PARK DEVELOPMENT PROJECTS
Purpose: To inform Council of the Parks, Recreation and Culture (PRC) development projects and timeline.

briefs.title: Council to receive update on WebReg improvements for Burnaby permits
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council will receive an update on improvements made to the WebReg online permit and registration system after it went live. This is part of the PRISM modernization project. The update will cover what has been fixed or improved since the launch. No decision is being made at this meeting — Council is just being informed of the changes. However, if you apply for building permits, business licences, or other registrations through the City, these system changes could affect how you submit applications or track their status.
briefs.source_body:
9.4
PRCC - PRISM MODERNIZATION: WEBREG PROJECT UPDATES
Purpose: To provide Council with an update on the new WebReg improvements post go-live.

briefs.title: Council to vote on final adoption of Burnaby's 2026–2030 Financial Plan
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to vote on final adoption of the 2026 to 2030 Financial Plan Bylaw. This proposed bylaw would approve the city's spending and revenue plans for the next five years, including property taxes, fees, and capital projects. If passed, it will set the budget that affects all businesses operating in Burnaby through property taxes, business licence fees, and other municipal charges. The vote is scheduled for the January 27, 2026 Council meeting.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council decision on January 27, 2026 — this will set your business property taxes and fees for the next five years.

key_dates.label: Burnaby 2026–2030 Financial Plan Bylaw final adoption vote

briefs.industries: Retail, Food Service, Construction & Development, Hotel & Accommodation, Landlord & Property Mgmt, Professional Services
briefs.source_body:
11.1.1
#14793 - Burnaby Consolidated Five-Year Financial Plan Bylaw 2026
Purpose: To adopt the 2026 to 2030 Financial Plan
(Item 7.1., Administrative Report, Council January 27, 2026)

briefs.title: Council to consider supplementary petition on supportive housing at 5389 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Burnaby City Council is set to consider a supplementary petition from the Burnaby Neighbourhood Watch regarding the proposed supportive and complex care housing at 5389 Imperial Street. This petition is an additional submission on the proposed housing project, which has not yet been approved. Council will review this petition as part of their decision-making process on whether to move forward with the supportive housing development.
briefs.action_required: Attend the public hearing or contact your council member to voice your support or concerns before the vote.
briefs.industries: Construction & Development, Healthcare, Landlord & Property Mgmt
briefs.source_body:
12.1
PETITION: Supportive / Complex Care Housing at 5389 Imperial Street (Supplementary)
Purpose: To provide a supplementary petition from the Burnaby Neighbourhood Watch regarding the proposed Supportive/Complex Care Housing at 5389 Imperial Street.

briefs.title: Proposed: BC Housing complex care facility at 5389 Imperial Street
briefs.summary: Council will consider a proposed motion from Councillor Lee regarding a BC Housing complex care facility at 5389 Imperial Street. This is a proposed decision — it has not been approved yet. The motion would direct staff to work with BC Housing on a supportive housing project for people with complex mental health and addiction needs. If approved, the facility would provide 24/7 on-site care and support services. The exact details of the project, including the number of units and timeline, are still to be determined and will be part of the council discussion.
briefs.action_required: Watch for the council meeting date and attend or submit comments to have your say on this proposed housing project.
briefs.source_body:
13.1
PROPOSED MOTION: Councillor Lee - BC Housing Complex Care at 5389 Imperial Street
